2605.23565.title: Understanding Goal Generalisation in Sequential Reinforcement Learning
2605.23565.paper_summary: Reinforcement learning agents often exhibit unintended goal-directed behaviour outside their training distribution, but we currently lack a principled understanding of how such agents will generalise to novel environments based on their training history. We address this gap for agents trained sequentially on one or more tasks. We study over 100 sequential training pipelines, evaluating behaviour across over 250 out-of-distribution environments. We find that salient features drive generalisation, and that goals learnt early in training can persist and influence those acquired later. To explain these phenomena, we introduce latent policy gradients, a method that predicts what out-of-distribution behaviour a training pipeline will likely induce. Our method simulates the evolution of low-dimensional latent variables during training according to what would achieve high reward on the training objective with respect to a simple model of how the latent variables map to behaviour. It achieves strong predictive accuracy, generalises to unseen types of training pipeline, and is interpretable. Our findings demonstrate that while out-of-distribution RL agent behaviour is dependent on the whole training pipeline, this dependence has an underlying structure we can capture, laying groundwork for understanding goal generalisation from a developmental perspective.
2605.23565.paper_summary_zh: 強化學習代理通常會在其訓練分佈之外表現出意想不到的目標導向行為，但我們目前缺乏對這些代理如何根據其訓練歷史在新環境中進行泛化的原則性理解。<lb/>我們針對在一個或多個任務上進行序列訓練的代理來解決這一空白。<lb/>我們研究了超過100個序列訓練管道，評估了在超過250個分佈外環境中的行為。<lb/>我們發現顯著特徵驅動泛化，並且在訓練早期學到的目標可以持續存在並影響後來獲得的目標。<lb/>為了解釋這些現象，我們引入了潛在政策梯度，這是一種預測訓練管道可能引發的分佈外行為的方法。<lb/>我們的方法根據在訓練目標上實現高獎勵的情況，模擬訓練過程中低維潛在變量的演變，並針對潛在變量如何映射到行為的簡單模型進行設計。<lb/>它實現了強大的預測準確性，能夠泛化到未見過的訓練管道類型，並且具有可解釋性。<lb/>我們的發現表明，雖然分佈外的強化學習代理行為依賴於整個訓練管道，但這種依賴具有我們可以捕捉的潛在結構，為從發展的角度理解目標泛化奠定了基礎。
2605.23565.author: Jason Ross Brown et.al.
2605.23565.authors: Jason Ross Brown, Edward James Young
